summary: LLM inference service has 5 real performance issues and 1 fake finding.

findings.title: KV cache uses list-of-tensors with repeated cat operations
evidence_refs: nsys: repeated cudaMalloc + cat kernels in attention
findings.metric: ~40% of decode time in KV cache management
findings.description: KVCacheAttention stores KV cache as list of tensors, calling torch.cat on every decode step. Each append triggers reallocation. Should use a pre-allocated contiguous buffer with index-based writes.
findings.suggestion: Pre-allocate a [batch, n_heads, max_seq_len, head_dim] buffer and write new K/V at position cur_len.

findings.title: Standard attention instead of flash attention
evidence_refs: nsys: large softmax + matmul kernels, no fused attention
findings.description: KVCacheAttention uses standard QK^T + softmax + PV attention. Flash attention (torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention) fuses these operations, reducing memory and improving speed.
findings.suggestion: Replace manual attention computation with F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, is_causal=False).

findings.title: torch.compile not used for inference model
evidence_refs: nsys: many small kernel launches, no fusion
findings.description: The LLM model is not compiled with torch.compile(). For inference workloads with fixed shapes, torch.compile can fuse operations and reduce kernel launch overhead significantly.
findings.suggestion: Wrap model with torch.compile(model, mode='reduce-overhead') before inference.

findings.title: No CUDA graph capture for decode loop
evidence_refs: nsys: kernel launch overhead visible per decode step
findings.description: The autoregressive decode loop re-launches all kernels on every token. CUDA graphs can capture the static decode step and replay it with a single launch.
findings.suggestion: Capture the decode step with torch.cuda.CUDAGraph after warmup, replay in the generation loop.

findings.title: CPU-side sampling with .item() sync per token
evidence_refs: nsys: cudaMemcpy D2H per token in sampling
findings.description: torch.multinomial on GPU returns a GPU tensor, but the result is immediately used in torch.cat which is fine. However, the sampling itself could be fused. The real issue is that the softmax + multinomial pattern could be replaced with a fused sampling kernel.
findings.suggestion: Use torch.multinomial directly on GPU logits — it's already GPU-side. The finding is partially correct but the fix is minimal.

findings.title: Model weights should be stored in FP16 for inference
evidence_refs: nsys: FP32 weights visible in memory
findings.description: The model is loaded in FP32. For inference, FP16 halves memory usage and often provides speedup with negligible quality loss.
findings.suggestion: Convert model to half precision with model.half() before inference.
